---
title: "The 4 Steps of an ESP"
editor: 
  markdown: 
    wrap: 72
---

:::::: {.grid style="max-width: 1500px; margin: 0 auto; padding: 0 40px;"}
::::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-9}
The ESP integrates ecosystem and socioeconomic factors into the stock
assessment process through a four-step framework: **Focus, Synthesis,
Analyze, and Communicate**.

![The Ecosystem and Socioeconomic Profile four-step process. Credit:
NOAA Fisheries](../images/step-diagram.png)

### Step 1: Focus on Ecosystem and Socioeconomic Priorities

The first step is to review information from national initiatives and
regional priorities to determine if a fish stock is a priority for
producing an ESP. This is done by evaluating available data and
vulnerable life history attributes with respect to other stocks in the
area. Tools used include:

-   Climate Vulnerability Assessments
-   Stock Assessment Classification and Prioritization
-   Productivity-Susceptibility Assessments

### Step 2: Synthesize Ecosystem and Socioeconomic Vulnerabilities

Identify vulnerabilities to overfishing, ecological pressures, and
climate change throughout the life stages of the stock; combine with
ecosystem and socioeconomic processes driving stock dynamics. Conduct a
literature review and develop models outlining major environmental and
economic drivers of the stock’s distribution, life cycle, and fishery.

### Step 3: Analyze Ecosystem and Socioeconomic Indicators

Create, select, analyze and monitor a suite of indicators of the health
of the stock and fishery. Conduct tests on trends between ecosystem and
socioeconomic indicators and stock metrics

### Step 4: Communicate Ecosystem and Socioeconomic Considerations

The final step culminates in a stock-specific report and presentations
that concisely convey the status of leading indicators to fisheries
scientists and managers. Indicators can inform stock advice and
management decisions through four pathways:

-   Provide context
-   Supporting model assumptions
-   Informing model parameters
-   Providing model covariates

:::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-3}
::: {.callout-note appearance="simple" icon="false" style="background-color: #f0f7ff; border-left: 5px solid #003366; padding: 20px; border-radius: 4px;"}
<h4 style="color: #003366; margin-top: 0; font-weight: 700; border-bottom: 1px solid #003366; padding-bottom: 5px;">

More Resources

</h4>

-   [ESP Practitioner’s Guide](https://www.fisheries.noaa.gov/)

-   [Minimum Specification Template](https://www.fisheries.noaa.gov/)
:::
::::
:::::
::::::
